title: Messaging Platform Frontend Integrations

classes.name: AgentChatMixin
methods: send_text(self, chat_id, content, **ctx)  # abstract / raises NotImplementedError
send_done(self, chat_id, raw_text, **ctx)  # calls build_done_text then send_text
run_agent(self, chat_id, text, **ctx)  # async loop: put_task → dequeue → send_done

classes.name: WeComApp
methods: send_text(self, chat_id, content)  # splits text; calls client.reply_stream per part
on_text(self, frame)  # dedup by msgid; auth check; dispatch handle_command or run_agent
on_enter_chat(self, frame)  # sends WELCOME via client.reply_welcome
start(self)  # creates WSClient, registers event handlers, calls client.connect_async()

classes.name: DingTalkApp
methods: _send_batch_message(self, chat_id, msg_key, msg_param)  # group: groupMessages/send; 1-1: oToMessages/batchSend
send_text(self, chat_id, content)  # splits; calls _send_batch_message with sampleMarkdown
on_message(self, content, sender_id, sender_name, conversation_type, conversation_id)  # auth check; dispatch handle_command or run_agent
start(self)  # creates DingTalkStreamClient, registers _DingTalkHandler, reconnect loop

classes.name: _DingTalkHandler
methods: process(self, message)  # extracts text/sender from ChatbotMessage; calls app.on_message(); returns AckMessage.STATUS_OK
classes.role: Bridge/adapter between DingTalk SDK callback and DingTalkApp.on_message()

classes.name: QQApp
methods: send_text(self, chat_id, content, *, msg_id=None, is_group=False)  # group: post_group_message; c2c: post_c2c_message; uses _next_msg_seq()
on_message(self, data, is_group=False)  # dedup by id; auth check; dispatch handle_command or run_agent
start(self)  # creates QQBot via _make_bot_class(self); calls client.start(appid, secret); reconnect loop

classes.name: QQBot
classes.note: Dynamically created inside _make_bot_class(app); not a top-level class definition

classes.name: GeneraticAgent
classes.role: Shared AI agent; composed into all frontend apps via AgentChatMixin

relationships: QQBot delegates to QQApp via closure (app.on_message)
common_patterns: All apps: override send_text() with platform-specific API call
All apps: message handlers check public_access(ALLOWED) for authorization
All apps: content starting with '/' → handle_command(); else → run_agent()
All apps: start() creates platform client + runs infinite reconnect loop
WeComApp & QQApp: deduplication via PROCESSED_IDS deque(maxlen=1000)
DingTalkApp: OAuth2 token caching with expiry in _get_access_token()

module_level_state.qq: APP_ID, APP_SECRET, ALLOWED, PROCESSED_IDS, SEQ_LOCK, MSG_SEQ

important_notes: All three frontend classes follow an identical structural pattern — platform-specific SDK connection wrapped around the shared AgentChatMixin base. _DingTalkHandler acts as an adapter translating the DingTalk SDK callback contract (CallbackHandler.process) into DingTalkApp.on_message(). QQBot is a factory-created inner class (closure over QQApp instance). WeComApp uses WebSocket streaming (reply_stream) vs DingTalk/QQ which use REST APIs.
